name: Farmhouse north of Andorhal
region: Cult-held Eastweald north of Andorhal on the approach toward Barov-held country

sensoryProfile.sight: A half-collapsed farmhouse, one hanging gate, dead apple trees, an orderly yard, amber grit on the well rim, and ritual scoring cut deep into the kitchen floor.
sensoryProfile.sound: Horse panic, a false stillness before the first crossbow crack, shattering plaster, and the rush of fire and smoke through the back of the house.
sensoryProfile.smell: Wet plaster, old straw, lamp smoke, worked magic packed too densely into a small room, then oil fire and blood.
sensoryProfile.touch: Cold flagstones, chalk-sunk ritual cuts, split bottle glass, and bandage cloth shoved hard against a fresh side wound.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: A site that looks merely convenient from the yard and reveals itself as active plague infrastructure the moment Jaina starts reading it.

establishedFacts.fact: The farmhouse is a fresh cult worksite rather than an abandoned ordinary ruin.
Its threshold carries an alarm line built to punish outside reading, and the interior binding work belongs to a larger mass-animation structure.
Useful ritual data can still be recovered here, but the site is also hot enough to draw a living ambush.

eventsHere.event: Jaina identifies the body as part of the plague lock and salvages incomplete binding data.
Living cult ambushers hit the house mid-read and Sylvanas is wounded pulling Jaina out.
The team escapes by turning the back of the room into fire and smoke instead of trying to hold the doorway.
